content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. Here, there are updates to the array and queries.**

Given 222 arrays AAA and BBB, both of length NNN, define f(A,B)f(A, B)f(A,B) as follows:


Choose 222 intervals [L1,R1][L_1, R_1][L1‚Äč,R1‚Äč], [L2,R2][L_2, R_2][L2‚Äč,R2‚Äč] such that:

1‚Č§Li‚Č§Ri‚Č§N1 \le L_i \le R_i \le N1‚Č§Li‚Äč‚Č§Ri‚Äč‚Č§N
[L1,R1]‚ą©[L2,R2][L_1, R_1] \cap [L_2, R_2][L1‚Äč,R1‚Äč]‚ą©[L2‚Äč,R2‚Äč] is non-empty, i.e. they share at least one point.


The score of this choice of intervals is defined as ‚ąĎi=L1R1Ai+‚ąĎi=L2R2Bi\displaystyle\sum_{i = L_1}^{R_1} A_i + \displaystyle\sum_{i = L_2}^{R_2} B_ii=L1‚Äč‚ąĎR1‚Äč‚ÄčAi‚Äč+i=L2‚Äč‚ąĎR2‚Äč‚ÄčBi‚Äč.
f(L,R)f(L, R)f(L,R) is defined as the maximum possible score under the above constraints.
Less formally, you have to choose intersecting subarrays from AAA and BBB, and maximize the total sum of the subarrays.

You are given 222 arrays AAA and BBB of length NNN. There are QQQ events, of 222 types:


**Updates:** You are given 333 integers PPP, XXX and YYY, and you should update APA_PAP‚Äč to XXX and BPB_PBP‚Äč to YYY.
**Queries:** You are given 222 integers LLL and RRR, and you should find f(A[L,R],B[L,R])f(A[L, R], B[L, R])f(A[L,R],B[L,R]) where A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] denotes the subarray [AL,AL+1,AL+2,‚Ä¶,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, A_{L + 2}, \ldots, A_R][AL‚Äč,AL+1‚Äč,AL+2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äč].

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and QQQ, the size of the arrays and the number of events.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN integers - B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.
The next QQQ lines each describe an event, first you are given a integer type\text{type}type denoting the type of the event.
If type=1\text{type} = 1type=1, you are given 333 integers PPP, XXX and YYY; describing an update.
If type=2\text{type} = 2type=2, you are given 222 integers LLL and RRR; describing a query.




Output Format
For each test case, for each query, output the value of f(A[L,R],B[L,R])f(A[L, R], B[L, R])f(A[L,R],B[L,R]).
constraints: For all updates, 1‚Č§P‚Č§N1 \le P \le N1‚Č§P‚Č§N and ‚ąí109‚Č§X,Y‚Č§109-10^9 \le X, Y \le 10^9‚ąí109‚Č§X,Y‚Č§109
For all queries, 1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N.
The sum of NNN and the sum of QQQ over all test cases both do not exceed 1.5‚čÖ1051.5 \cdot 10^51.5‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:
Event 111, Query: We can choose [L1,R1]=[1,2][L_1, R_1] = [1, 2][L1‚Äč,R1‚Äč]=[1,2] and [L2,R2]=[2,3][L_2, R_2] = [2, 3][L2‚Äč,R2‚Äč]=[2,3], and the score is 1+3+3+1=81 + 3 + 3 + 1 = 81+3+3+1=8.
Event 222, Query: We need to solve for the range [L,R]=[2,2][L, R] = [2, 2][L,R]=[2,2], i.e. the corresponding subarrays are [3][3][3] and [3][3][3]. Here we choose [L1,R1]=[1,1][L_1, R_1] = [1, 1][L1‚Äč,R1‚Äč]=[1,1] and [L2,R2]=[1,1][L_2, R_2] = [1, 1][L2‚Äč,R2‚Äč]=[1,1] to get a sum of 666.
Event 333, Update: AAA is updated to [1,‚ąí2,‚ąí1][1, -2, -1][1,‚ąí2,‚ąí1] and BBB is updated to [‚ąí1,‚ąí5,1][-1, -5, 1][‚ąí1,‚ąí5,1].
Event 444, Query: We can choose [L1,R1]=[1,1][L_1, R_1] = [1, 1][L1‚Äč,R1‚Äč]=[1,1] and [L2,R2]=[1,1][L_2, R_2] = [1, 1][L2‚Äč,R2‚Äč]=[1,1] which gets 1+(‚ąí1)=01 + (-1) = 01+(‚ąí1)=0. It is not possible to get a better sum after the update.
